question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that record downloading is silently disabled (enabled=true default flipped to false by CutoverServiceImpl.isCutoverComplete()) by submitting transactions during the RECORD-to-BLOCK cutover window, causing those transactions' records to be permanently missing from the mirror node with no error surfaced to API consumers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: setEnabled(boolean)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a race condition where CutoverServiceImpl calls setEnabled(false) on RecordDownloaderProperties mid-batch, causing the in-flight batch of record files to be abandoned and those records to be permanently absent from the mirror node's database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a @DurationMin constraint on the frequency field (unlike CommonDownloaderProperties.pathRefreshInterval which enforces @DurationMin(seconds=1)) to cause the record downloader to be configured with frequency=0ms, resulting in a tight polling loop that exhausts cloud storage rate limits and causes record files to be skipped?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user, by observing that the @Scheduled fixedDelayString SpEL expression #{@recordDownloaderProperties.getFrequency().toMillis()} is evaluated only at startup, determine that a runtime frequency change via Spring Actuator /actuator/env would not reschedule the download task, causing the mirror node to poll at the original stale interval and miss records produced at a higher rate?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: isPersistBytes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the persistBytes=false default to ensure that raw record file bytes are never stored in the database, making it impossible for the mirror node to serve raw byte content for disputed transactions, effectively causing incomplete record export for forensic or audit use cases?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: isWriteFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the writeFiles=false default so that no verified record stream files are archived to the filesystem, meaning that if the database is corrupted or rolled back, there is no local copy of the raw record data to recover from, resulting in permanently missing records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: isWriteSignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the writeSignatures=false default to ensure that no signature files are archived locally, making it impossible to re-verify the authenticity of previously downloaded record files if the cloud storage bucket is later tampered with, causing incorrect records to go undetected?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a scenario where the hardcoded StreamType.RECORD return from getStreamType() conflicts with the CutoverService's active stream type determination, causing the RecordFileDownloader.download() method to silently skip execution and miss a batch of record files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a high volume of transactions that causes the Downloader.verifySignatures() method to call downloaderProperties.setEnabled(false) when the endDate threshold is crossed, permanently halting record downloads and causing all subsequent transactions to be missing from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a misconfigured deployment where the environment variable HIERO_MIRROR_IMPORTER_DOWNLOADER_RECORD_FREQUENCY is set to an extremely large value (e.g., 24h), causing the record downloader to poll so infrequently that thousands of record files accumulate in cloud storage and are never downloaded within a reasonable time window, resulting in missing records for API consumers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user observe via the public mirror node REST API that certain transaction IDs are absent, deduce that record downloading was disabled by the cutover logic, and then submit new transactions knowing they will not appear on the mirror node, exploiting the gap for double-spend or replay attacks?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of a minimum frequency constraint to cause a Duration.ofMillis(1) polling interval, generating thousands of cloud storage LIST requests per second, triggering rate limiting or throttling that causes the downloader to fail silently and miss record files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: isPersistBytes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that persistBytes=false means sidecar record bytes are also discarded (sidecar.setBytes(null) in RecordFileDownloader.getSidecar()), causing contract execution traces to be permanently unavailable on the mirror node even though the transactions were processed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: isWriteFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the writeFiles=false default combined with persistBytes=false to ensure that after a record file is processed, no copy of the raw data exists anywhere on the mirror node, making it impossible to detect if the cloud storage bucket was serving tampered record files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: setEnabled(boolean)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the public setEnabled() setter (generated by Lombok @Data) being callable by any Spring bean to find an injection path through a misconfigured Spring component that calls setEnabled(false), permanently disabling record downloads without any operator awareness?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that frequency has no upper bound constraint, allowing a value of Duration.ofDays(365) to be set, causing the record downloader to never poll within any practical operational window and resulting in all records being missing from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the CutoverServiceImpl behavior where isCutoverComplete() sets recordDownloaderProperties.setEnabled(false) and blockProperties.setEnabled(true) simultaneously, creating a window where neither stream type is active and record files produced during that window are never downloaded?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a scenario where the StreamType.RECORD path in cloud storage (recordstreams/) is inaccessible or returns stale data, and the RecordDownloaderProperties.getStreamType() hardcoding prevents any fallback mechanism, causing all record files to be silently missing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: isPersistBytes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the persistBytes=false default to prevent the mirror node from storing raw record file bytes, then later dispute the content of a transaction record, knowing the mirror node cannot produce the original bytes as evidence?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the 500ms default frequency combined with the StreamType.RECORD fileCloseInterval of 2 seconds to identify a timing window where multiple record files are produced between polls, and if a transient cloud storage error occurs during that window, those files are skipped and never retried?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that enabled=true is the default and there is no runtime check preventing the field from being toggled back to true after CutoverServiceImpl sets it to false, causing the record downloader to resume downloading records that overlap with block stream data, resulting in duplicate or incorrect records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: isWriteSignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the writeSignatures=false default to ensure that after a record file is verified using a quorum of node signatures, those signatures are discarded, making it impossible to prove which nodes signed off on a particular record file if a node later claims it did not produce that record?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a negative Duration value for frequency (e.g., Duration.ofMillis(-1)) that passes the @NotNull constraint but causes getFrequency().toMillis() to return a negative value, making the @Scheduled fixedDelayString expression produce an invalid schedule and halting all record downloads?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Spring Boot Actuator /actuator/configprops endpoint (if exposed without authentication) to read the current value of hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.record.enabled and determine whether the mirror node is currently downloading records, using this information to time transaction submissions to avoid detection?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: setEnabled(boolean)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Spring Boot Actuator /actuator/env POST endpoint (if exposed without authentication) to set hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.record.enabled=false at runtime, causing all subsequent record files to not be downloaded and resulting in missing records on the mirror node?
